title: ageratina espinosarum var. subintegrifolia
intro: Ageratina espinosarum var. subintegrifolia, also known as Hierba de la lagrima or Tear Herb, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its shrub-like appearance and small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Ageratina espinosarum var. subintegrifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include relief from gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as its potential to help manage symptoms of respiratory diseases like bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Ageratina espinosarum var. subintegrifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
